data.title: Raw sugar hits five-month high as oil prices resume rally
data.description:
Raw sugar futures on ICE hit a five-month high on Thursday as oil prices resumed their rally amid uncertainty among investors over prospects of an end to the U.S.-Israel war on Iran.

A sustained rise in energy prices can prompt cane mills in top grower Brazil and elsewhere to lower sugar output in favour of ethanol, a cane-based biofuel.

SUGAR

* Raw sugar ​​settled up 0.32 cent, or 2.1%, at 15.87 cents per lb​, having hit a five-month high of 15.97 cents earlier.

* Contradictory signals earlier from both the U.S. and Iran on negotiations to end the war in the energy-rich Middle East left markets on edge, with global stock and bond markets falling amid the oil price surge.

* Brazil is expected to cut sugar exports in the 2026/27 season by 14.2% as mills divert sugarcane to make ethanol due to high energy prices, consultancy Safras & Mercado said on Thursday.

* AP commodities said that while sugar is under pressure from modest surpluses and heavy speculative selling, relief rallies, even if temporary, are likely to emerge given the sweetener's sensitivity to weather risks and ethanol economics.

* The consultancy added that speculators' short positions, or bets on prices falling, is large, meaning there is a risk many will reverse course and bet on price gains if their position remains in the red for much longer.

* White sugar rose 1.2% to $459.60 a ton.

COFFEE

* Arabica coffee settled down 8.45 cents, or 2.7%, at $3.0765 per lb​​, having set a seven-week high of $3.1950 on Tuesday.

* The weather anomaly known as El Nino is likely to develop globally around the third quarter of this year and is set to bring dryness and higher temperatures by early 2027 to tropical areas where coffee and cocoa grow, trader Sucafina said.

* Robusta coffee slipped 0.9% to $3,596 a ton.

* Dealers in top robusta grower Vietnam said farmers are not selling at the moment and that those needing to fulfil contracts have begun turning to Brazil and Indonesia for beans.

COCOA

* London cocoa ​settled up 28 pounds, or 1.2%, at 2,356 pounds per ton.

* Cocoa is under pressure from weak demand and a generally favourable outlook for crops in West Africa, though much of that is priced in for now.

* New York cocoa rose 1% to $3,164 a ton, after sliding 3.2% on Wednesday.

data.title: Goslings & Bisous Launch Limited-Edition Dark 'n Stormy(R) Rumrunners Sunglasses at Palm Beach International Boat Show
data.description:
A limited-edition eyewear release celebrating the art of leisure, and the ritual of the Dark 'n Stormy®

PALM BEACH, FL / ACCESS Newswire / March 26, 2026 / Bisous, the eyewear brand known for craftsmanship and coastal sophistication, announces the launch of the Dark 'n Stormy® Rumrunners, a limited-edition sunglasses collaboration with Goslings, the Official Rum of the Palm Beach International Boat Show. Debuting during the Boat Show, which begins on March 25, this exclusive release celebrates a unique partnership rooted in craftsmanship, style, and the spirit of leisure.

The Dark 'n Stormy® Rumrunners were designed to evoke the iconic cocktail itself. The upper frame features a rich brown tone reminiscent of Goslings Rum, while the lower frame glows with a golden hue inspired by Goslings Stormy Ginger Beer. Each pair comes with two custom lens cloths and a cocktail card to guide owners in crafting their own Dark 'n Stormy® at home, making the experience as immersive as it is stylish.

"The Dark 'n Stormy® Rumrunners are the stylish result of a fun collaboration with Bisous," said Malcolm L. Gosling, eighth-generation rum maker and President of Goslings Export Bermuda.

"Thoughtfully crafted and rich in character, these frames have translated the essence of the Goslings brand into eyewear designed for the life of leisure, on the water, and beyond."

Production is strictly limited to 100 pairs, emphasizing the collectible nature of the release. The Rumrunners combine thoughtful design with enduring style, perfect for afternoons on the water, sun-soaked events, or any occasion where leisure is celebrated.

The Dark 'n Stormy® Rumrunners will be available exclusively from Bisous beginning March 25 at the Palm Beach Boat Show ​​and can be purchased online and in limited quantities at www.iwearbisous.com.

About Bisous

Bisous is an independent eyewear brand dedicated to creating thoughtfully designed sunglasses that combine timeless style with exceptional materials. Bisous was founded on the belief that great products should look good and be built to last. The brand produces small batches of distinctive frames crafted from premium Italian acetate and quality hardware.

Designed to complement moments of leisure as well as the pursuits of those who like to push the limits, Bisous sunglasses balance refinement, durability, and enduring style.

Bisous sunglasses are available online and through select retail partners and collaborations.

For more information about Bisous, visit www.iwearbisous.com and follow @iwearbisous

About Goslings Rum

A family business for over 200 years, Goslings Rum is Bermuda's largest export product and the only company that blends and bottles rum on the island. First exported to the United States in 1980, Goslings offers distinctive, award-winning rums: Goslings Black Seal Rum, Goslings 151 Proof Black Seal Rum, Goslings Spirited Seas Ocean Aged Rum, Goslings Family Reserve Old Rum, and Goslings Papa Seal Single Barrel Bermuda Rum. Goslings Stormy Ginger Beer, crafted specifically for use in the trademarked Goslings Dark 'n Stormy® cocktail, is a top- selling ginger beer in the United States, and expanded in 2023 to include Stormy Peach Ginger Beer. Goslings is available in all 50 states as well as North American and European Duty Free, the Caribbean, Canada, Australia, and most European markets. For more information about Goslings Rum, visit Goslings.com and follow @GoslingsRum.

Media Contact:

Sarah Shriver Smothers

sarah@fordhamilton.com

SOURCE: Goslings Rum

View the original press release on ACCESS Newswire

data.title: Sheinbaum Asks Retailers to Forgo Diesel Price Increases after Government Trims Fuel Taxes — OPIS
data.description:
Mexican President Claudia Sheinbaum on Thursday urged fuel retailers to refrain from raising the retail price of diesel, citing a recent reduction in federal taxes on the fuel as oil prices surge on the continuing Middle East conflict.

Sheinbaum said the government reduced the special tax on fuels, or IEPS, for diesel to 2.8129 pesos per liter (15cts/liter) this week, from 4.771 pesos/liter in the previous week. While the move was designed to limit higher prices for drivers, some retailers are charging more than 30 pesos/liter, she added.

Although Sheinbaum said a "voluntary agreement" to keep diesel prices in check remains in place, market participants said the announcement was "more of a warning signal." The added that while there were meetings on the issue this week between the government and fuel suppliers, no agreement was reached.

The president said she told retailers that the average diesel price in Mexico of about 28.5 pesos/liter, "is still very high."

One market participant suggested that the recent increase in retail diesel prices came as retailers drew down lower-cost inventories and are now replacing them with higher-priced fuel.

Since the U.S. and Israel attacked Iran in late February, the average landed spot price of the U.S. Gulf Coast ULSD on Mexico's East Coast has climbed sharply. It reached a high of 19.219 pesos/liter on March 19, up 84.5% from a month ago and 74% above the year-ago average price.

Spot ULSD prices averaged 15.861 pesos/liter from Feb. 25 to Wednesday, compared with 11.278 pesos/liter over the same period of 2025.

One large fuel distributor said the reduction in the retail diesel tax, along with some lower rack prices, has helped to limit retail prices increases, while adding that Pemex price changes have lagged behind those in the international market.

The source added that there is still room to remain competitive with Pemex, except for regular gasoline.

The Mexican government earlier this month renewed a nearly yearlong voluntary agreement with retailers to cap the pump price of regular gasoline at 24 pesos/liter.

This content was created by Oil Price Information Service, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. OPIS is run independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

  • Reporting by Karla Omaña, komana@opisnet.com and José Luis Adriano, jadriano@opisnet.com; Editing by Jeffrey Barber, jbarber@opisnet.com

data.title: Credo Technology Settles With Molex Over Patent Dispute
data.description:
By Katherine Hamilton

Credo Technology settled with electronic-part maker Molex to drop its patent disputes.

The connectivity provider said Thursday it reached a cross-license and settlement agreement with Molex. All lawsuits will be dismissed, bringing an end to any patent disputes, as part of the deal.

Credo filed a complaint in March of last year alleging patent infringement against multiple companies, including Molex. The claims were over Credo's patents for active electrical cables.

The company asked the U.S. International Trade Commission to investigate Molex and the other companies. It simultaneously filed complaints in federal district court.

In August 2025, Credo settled with the other three companies involved in its complaint.

Write to Katherine Hamilton at katherine.hamilton@wsj.com

data.title: Ashford Hospitality Trust says liquidation value of non-traded preferred stock is $25.00 per share
data.description:
Ashford Hospitality Trust reported an estimated liquidation value of $25.00 per share for its non-traded preferred stock as of December 31, 2025.

Key Highlights:

  • Independent valuation by Robert A. Stanger concluded liquidation value equals $25.00 per share, matching each series' liquidation preference.
  • Valuation approaches included market cap, direct capitalization, third-party appraisals; equity value exceeded total preferred liquidation preference.
  • No analyst target prices were available, and Stanger noted valuations rely on assumptions that could materially differ under alternate methods.
  • Report excludes post‑Dec 31, 2025 transactions and is not an audited GAAP fair‑value determination for trading on an exchange.

Original SEC Filing: ASHFORD HOSPITALITY TRUST INC [ AHT ] - 8-K - Mar. 26, 2026

data.title: Alpha Cognition reports $10.2M revenue in FY2025, $6.8M product sales; $20.7M net loss
data.description:
Alpha Cognition reported full-year 2025 revenue of $10.2 million, including $6.8 million of net product sales from ZUNVEYL, and recorded a net loss of $20.7 million. The company ended the year with $66.0 million in cash and cash equivalents and a forecasted cash runway of roughly two years following an October 2025 capital raise. Alpha Cognition highlighted growing commercial traction for ZUNVEYL with rising bottles dispensed, an expanding prescriber base and a second national PBM agreement.

Financial Highlights

  • Total revenues for the year ended December 31, 2025: $10.2 million (product sales $6.8M; licensing $3.4M).
  • Net product sales (ZUNVEYL) for 2025: $6.8 million; Q4 2025 net product revenue approximately $2.5 million.
  • Cost of product sales (excluding amortization) for 2025: $474,006; cost of licensing revenue: $1,441,317.
  • Operating loss for 2025: $22.7 million; Net loss for 2025: $20.7 million (net loss per share, basic: $(1.17)).
  • Cash and cash equivalents as of December 31, 2025: $66.0 million (compared with $48.5M at year-end 2024); company completed a $40M net proceeds capital raise in October 2025.

Business Highlights

  • Commercial traction for ZUNVEYL: Q4 2025 bottles dispensed were 4,941, a 62% quarter-over-quarter increase; December was the strongest month with 1,859 bottles.
  • Prescriber and facility adoption: Q4 prescribers grew 50% over the prior quarter; cumulative homes with prescriptions rose 26% in Q4; sales team engaged with ~4,000 unique facilities since launch and reached 1,986 homes in the quarter.
  • Payer progress: Executed a second national PBM agreement, giving contracts with two of the four major PBMs relevant to long-term care.
  • Clinical and regulatory activities: Ongoing enrollment in the BEACON real-world effectiveness study; plans to initiate CONVERGE (retrospective analysis of ~400 long-term care patients) and RESOLVE (phase 4 tolerability/behavior study) in AD patients.
  • Pipeline and IP: Issuance of an additional U.S. patent covering ZUNVEYL dosing regimens; plans to complete preclinical work and advance a sublingual formulation into clinical testing in 2026 targeting patients with dysphagia or aphagia.

Original SEC Filing: Alpha Cognition Inc. [ ACOG ] - 8-K - Mar. 26, 2026

Disclaimer
This is an AI-powered summary. It may contain inaccuracies. Consider verifying important information with the source. Please note this summary is solely based on documents filed with the SEC.

data.title: Ontology to Host Monthly Discord Quiz on March 27th
data.description:
Ontology announced that its monthly Discord quiz will take place on March 27 at 09:30 UTC. According to the post, participants can expect trivia, community activities, and ONG prizes. The event is open to both long-time community members and newcomers.

Refer to the official tweet by ONT:

Ontology - The Trust Layer for Web3
@OntologyNetwork

Our monthly Discord quiz is back! Join us at 9:30am UTC this Friday for trivia, good vibes, and bragging rights... oh, and ONG Prizes!

All welcome, whether you're an Ontology OG or just getting started.https://t.co/M87OtGCF0d pic.twitter.com/Khd2e8MWI6

Mar 26, 2026

ONT Info

Ontology is an open-source blockchain that provides Web3 trust, privacy and security to enterprises and individual users.

Ontology functions as a blockchain framework that supports public blockchain systems and offers customization capabilities for specific applications. It facilitates interaction between blockchains with different protocol groups, contributing to the development of shared modules of underlying infrastructure for various distributed scenarios, including digital identification and distributed data exchange.

The ONT token was created as part of Ontology's two-tier token system. ONT serves as a governance token used for voting on network decisions, while ONG (Ontology Gas) is a utility token used for transaction fees and executing smart contracts on the network.

data.title: Nasdaq falls into correction territory and Trump pauses plans to attack Iranian energy infrastructure
data.description:
By Joseph Adinolfi and Christine Idzelis

Trump said he would pause attacking Iran's energy infrastructure for 10 days late on Thursday as stocks tumbled

President Trump announced a 10-day pause on attacks against Iran's energy infrastructure after the bell Thursday.

U.S. stocks tumbled on Thursday, with the S&P 500 index and Nasdaq Composite wiping out their gains from earlier in the week. The Nasdaq also finished in correction territory for the first time in a year, according to Dow Jones Market Data.

As of Thursday's close, the Nasdaq COMP dropped more than 10% from its record high reached on Oct. 29, meeting the traditional definition of entering a correction. The S&P 500 SPX registered a decline of 1.7%, its biggest daily decline since the start of the Iran war. The index finished at 6,477.16, its lowest level since Sept. 3 and 7.2% off its record high.

The Dow Jones Industrial Average DJIA also struggled, extending its month-to-date loss to beyond 6%. Dow Jones Market Data showed the blue-chip average was on track for its biggest monthly percentage-point drop since September 2022, when it fell 8.8%.

A few minutes after the closing bell, President Trump took to Truth Social to announce that he had paused plans to attack Iranian energy infrastructure until April 6. Contrary to media reports, talks with Iran were going "very well," Trump wrote. A number of factors were weighing on markets on Thursday.

A Reuters report that Iranian hardliners were pushing for Tehran to obtain a nuclear bomb helped push stocks to their lowest levels of the session in the afternoon, according to commentary published by Mizuho Securities trading desk. Another weak Treasury auction - this time, for $44 billion in seven-year notes - also contributed to pain in both the bond and stock markets. The auction was the latest in a series of disappointing U.S. debt sales held this week.

As a result, the yield on the 10-year Treasury note BX:TMUBMUSD10Y rose 8.8 basis points to 4.415%, the highest 3 p.m. level since July, Dow Jones Market Data showed. Bond yields move inversely with bond prices.

Investors are laser-focused on rising oil prices and bond yields, while keeping an eye out for any headline that might signal the U.S. is moving closer to a resolution with Iran, said Sam Stovall, chief investment strategist at CFRA Research. Brent crude (BRN00), the global benchmark, has continued to trade above $100 a barrel, and stood at around $108 a barrel on Thursday when the U.S. stock market closed.

"Everybody is well aware that the longer oil prices stay elevated, the greater the likelihood that we could slip into an economic slowdown or a recession," Stovall said during an interview with MarketWatch.

Prices of gold (GC00) and silver (SI00) continued to slide Thursday as well.

At the same time, the Cboe Volatility Index VIX, otherwise known as the VIX or Wall Street's "fear gauge," was screaming higher. The index finished above 28 on Thursday, its highest end-of-day level since March 6.

Meta Platforms (META) was a notable loser on Thursday, falling nearly 8% and heaping even more pressure on the Nasdaq. A pair of back-to-back courtroom losses in California and New Mexico found the company liable for failing to protect young people using its platform.

While the Dow and small-cap Russell 2000 RUT were still clinging to weekly gains, both the S&P 500 and Nasdaq were on track for a fifth-straight weekly loss.

"We're in a period of really high geopolitical uncertainty," said Phil Neuhart, head of market and economic research at First Citizens Bank, in an interview Thursday.

By the time the closing bell rang, the Dow had fallen 469.38 points, or 1%, to 45,960.11. The Nasdaq Composite was down by 2.4% at 21,408.

-Joseph Adinolfi -Christine Idzelis

This content was created by MarketWatch, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. MarketWatch is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

data.title: Bank of Mexico Cuts Benchmark Interest Rate in Split Decision After Pause — Update
data.description:
By Anthony Harrup

MEXICO CITY--The Bank of Mexico lowered its benchmark interest rate Thursday in a split decision and said it will assess the timing for an additional cut in the future.

The five-member board of governors voted 3-2 to cut the overnight interest-rate target by a quarter of a percentage point to 6.75%, resuming monetary easing after a pause in February. Deputy governors Jonathan Heath and Galia Borja voted to leave the rate at 7%.

"Looking ahead, depending on the evolution of macroeconomic and financial conditions, the board will evaluate the appropriateness and timing for an additional reference rate cut," the central bank said.

Analysts were divided in their views as to whether the central bank would extend the pause given the recent pickup in inflation and possible fallout from the conflict in the Middle East, or resume the monetary easing cycle.

The 3-2 split in the vote, which was the closest since the easing cycle began, "reflects a board navegating a genuinely complex external environment" and sets the bar considerably higher for the next rate cut, Finamex Casa de Bolsa's chief economist Víctor Gómez Ayala said in a note.

The Bank of Mexico said uncertainty around the conflict in the Middle East poses downside risks to economic growth, and adds a layer of uncertainty to the inflation outlook. The bank raised its inflation forecasts for the first three quarters of this year, but still expects inflation to reach its 3% target in the second quarter of 2027.

Mexico's inflation rose to 4.63% in mid-March from 4.02% in February, led by a jump in fresh fruit and vegetable prices. Core inflation, which excludes energy and fresh food, slipped to 4.46% from 4.5%.

The Bank of Mexico said there was no evidence of second-round inflationary effects from the higher taxes on soda and tobacco that took effect in January, or the increase in tariffs on imports from countries with which Mexico doesn't have a trade agreement.

"The Bank of Mexico does not seem particularly concerned about the global energy shock and its impact on Mexico's inflation outlook," Capital Economics senior emerging markets economist Liam Peach said in a note. "In our view, the central bank is in no rush to cut interest rates again, but it still sees scope to do so."

The war in the Persian Gulf has virtually halted shipping through the Strait of Hormuz and pushed global oil prices to three-and-a-half year highs.

The Mexican government this week lowered taxes on gasoline after renewing an agreement with retailers to keep the price of regular gasoline under 24 pesos a liter, which is equivalent to about $5.10 a gallon. Tax relief for diesel was reinstated the previous week and increased this week.

Write to Anthony Harrup at anthony.harrup@wsj.com

data.title: Worksport 2025 10-K: $16.1M Revenue, $(3.16) Loss per Share
data.description:
Worksport reported fiscal 2025 net sales of $16.10 million, an 89.8% increase year-over-year, while delivering a net loss of $19.35 million and loss per share of $(3.16). Revenue growth was driven by higher online and distributor sales, but the company recorded a wider operating loss as selling, general and administrative and marketing expenses rose. Gross profit improved to $4.47 million with a 28% margin, reflecting higher production volumes and better absorption of overhead.

Financial Highlights

  • Net sales: $16.10 million, increased 89.8% vs. 2024 driven by higher online & distributor sales
  • Gross profit: $4.47 million; gross margin improved to 28% from 11%
  • Loss from operations: $(18.81) million; operating loss widened by $3.35 million due to higher G&A and sales & marketing spend
  • Net loss: $(19.35) million, increased loss vs. $(16.16) million in 2024
  • Loss per share (basic and diluted): $(3.16), improved vs. $(5.84) due to a larger weighted average shares outstanding

Business Highlights

  • Revenue growth and product mix: Net sales rose ~90% year-over-year, driven by increased tonneau cover sales and an expanded product offering.
  • Channel shift: Strong movement to direct-to-consumer and online retailers; online sales grew 142% and accounted for 74% of revenue in 2025.
  • Distributor expansion: Distributor sales surged 884% following new wholesale partner agreements and efforts to pursue national dealer networks.
  • Brand and marketing: Significant increase in sales and marketing spend to build brand awareness and reduce customer acquisition costs over time.
  • Operational scaling: Transitioned to hard-cover production, increased inventory and capital expenditures to support domestic manufacturing and higher production volumes.

Original SEC Filing: Worksport Ltd [ WKSP ] - 10-K - Mar. 26, 2026

Disclaimer
This is an AI-powered summary. It may contain inaccuracies. Consider verifying important information with the source. Please note this summary is solely based on documents filed with the SEC.

data.title: Rein Therapeutics 10-K: $0 Revenue, $0 Gross Profit; Net Loss $49.9M
data.description:
Rein Therapeutics reported zero product revenue and no gross profit for the year ended December 31, 2025, and recorded a net loss of $49.871 million reflecting ongoing development-stage operations. The company did not disclose an EPS figure in the provided filing data. The results underscore Rein Therapeutics' continued focus on clinical development rather than commercial sales.

Financial Highlights

  • Revenue: $0 million — the company has not generated any revenue from product sales.
  • Gross Profit: $0 million — no product sales reported, therefore no gross profit presented.
  • Operating Income (Loss): $(50.631) million — total operating expenses of $50.631 million.
  • Net Income (Net Loss): $(49.871) million — net loss for the year ended December 31, 2025.
  • Net Income Per Share: Not disclosed in the provided data.

Business Highlights

  • Clinical Progress: Initiated and dosed the first patient in the RENEW Phase 2 trial of lead peptide LTI-03 for idiopathic pulmonary fibrosis (IPF); interim topline data is expected in Q4 2026.
  • Pipeline Focus: Company resources have been refocused on LTI-03; development of LTI-01 and preclinical fibrosis programs have been paused indefinitely.
  • Regulatory Momentum: Received MHRA clearance and EMA authorization to run Phase 2 sites, and the EMA granted orphan drug designation for LTI-03.
  • Cost Discipline & Operations: Research & development and general & administrative spending declined year-over-year due to program delays and workforce changes while prioritizing the LTI-03 program.

Original SEC Filing: Rein Therapeutics, Inc. [ RNTX ] - 10-K - Mar. 26, 2026

Disclaimer

data.title: Rani Therapeutics 2025 10-K: $1.63M revenue, $(0.45) EPS
data.description:
Rani Therapeutics reported contract revenue of $1.633 million for the fiscal year and a net loss per Class A share of $(0.45), reflecting a net loss of $(40.95) million as shown in the consolidated statements. Operating loss improved to $(38.31) million year‑over‑year, while cash, cash equivalents and marketable securities totaled $49.709 million—management expects this funding to cover at least the next 12 months.

Financial Highlights

  • Revenue: Contract revenue of $1.633 million, up 58.9% versus 2024.
  • Operating income (loss): Loss from operations of $(38.310) million, improved 28.1% versus 2024.
  • Net income (loss): Net loss of $(40.950) million, a 27.6% improvement versus 2024.
  • Net income per Class A common share: $(0.45); weighted-average Class A shares: 66.138 million.
  • Cash position: Cash, cash equivalents and marketable securities of $49.709 million; management expects funding for at least 12 months.

Business Highlights

  • Revenue drivers & partnerships: Revenue increase was driven by collaboration activity, including a $10 million upfront from Chugai and related evaluation services.
  • Product development: Advanced the RaniPill platform—completed three Phase 1 trials for RaniPill GO and initiated a Phase 1 trial for RaniPill HC (RT-114) in December 2025.
  • Manufacturing and operations: Continued vertical integration with in‑house design and prototyping and investments in automated production lines to enable scale and protect intellectual property.
  • Cost management and R&D focus: Year‑over‑year declines in R&D and G&A due to cost containment and pausing or terminating certain programs; R&D spending is expected to increase as development programs resume.
  • Commercial strategy: Pursuing partnerships for development and commercialization (notably with Chugai) while retaining the option to build commercial infrastructure if products receive approval.

Original SEC Filing: Rani Therapeutics Holdings, Inc. [ RANI ] - 10-K - Mar. 26, 2026

Disclaimer

data.title: Quoin Pharmaceuticals 10-K: Net loss $15.8M, Cash $18.7M, EPS pressure
data.description:
Quoin Pharmaceuticals reported a net loss of $15.8 million for the year ended Dec 31, 2025, compared with a $9.0 million loss in 2024, driven primarily by higher research and development spending; the filing notes cash and investments of $18.7 million as of Dec 31, 2025 and states there is substantial doubt about the company’s ability to continue as a going concern. Revenue and EPS figures were not disclosed in the report. Higher R&D activity and increased operating costs underpin the deterioration in results and the company’s liquidity concerns.

Financial Highlights

  • Net loss: $(15.8) million for year ended Dec 31, 2025 (vs $(9.0) million in 2024).
  • Total operating expenses: $16.29 million for year ended Dec 31, 2025 (vs $9.53 million in 2024).
  • Research and development expense: $9.80 million for year ended Dec 31, 2025 (vs $3.60 million in 2024).
  • General and administrative expense: $6.49 million for year ended Dec 31, 2025 (vs $5.93 million in 2024).
  • Cash and investments: $18.7 million total as of Dec 31, 2025 (cash $3.8M; investments $14.9M); filing indicates substantial doubt about going concern.

Business Highlights

  • Clinical progress: QRX003 has entered pivotal registrational testing with U.S. and international sites opened; investigator-initiated pediatric studies are ongoing.
  • Product pipeline: Advancing QRX003 for Netherton Syndrome and Peeling Skin Syndrome; topical rapamycin formulations are progressing with target loadings achieved.
  • Commercial partnerships: Secured nine licensing/distribution partnerships covering 61 countries outside core U.S./EU/Japan territories to support broader future commercialization.
  • Operational scale-up: Increased R&D spending and site activations to support late-stage trials; manufacturing and clinical supply activities have expanded materially to support those trials.
  • Liquidity impact on operations: Cash constraints and the going-concern qualification may affect the timing of trials and commercialization plans, creating execution risk for ongoing and planned operational activities.

Original SEC Filing: Quoin Pharmaceuticals, Ltd. [ QNRX ] - 10-K - Mar. 26, 2026

Disclaimer
This is an AI-powered summary. It may contain inaccuracies. Consider verifying important information with the source. Please note this summary is solely based on documents filed with the SEC.

data.title: Chaince Digital Holdings 10-K: $1.87M Revenue, widened net loss of $5.10M
data.description:
Chaince Digital Holdings reported $1.867 million in revenue for the year, a 278% increase driven by expanded advisory and PIPE services, while the company recorded a net loss of $5.098 million reflecting discontinued mining losses and impairments. Gross profit improved to $1.201 million with a 64.31% margin, but operating losses widened as the company scaled general and administrative expenses. The results reflect a strategic shift toward financial services following regulatory approval and organizational changes during the year.

Financial Highlights

  • Revenue: $1.867 million, increase of $1.373 million (277.93%) versus prior year, driven by expanded advisory & PIPE services.
  • Gross Profit: $1.201 million; gross margin 64.31%, an improvement of 16.86 percentage points versus prior year.
  • Operating Loss: $(3.772) million, widened $1.771 million (88.53%) versus prior year due to higher G&A and scaling costs.
  • Net Loss: $(5.098) million, increased $0.563 million (12.43%), which includes losses and impairments from discontinued mining operations.
  • Net Income Per Share: Omitted in Part II Item 7/Item 8 and therefore not provided.

Business Highlights

  • Revenue Growth and Services Mix: Significant year-over-year revenue growth concentrated in financial services and advisory, primarily from expanded advisory and PIPE services.
  • Strategic Shift: Pivoted primary focus to financial services after FINRA approval of Chaince Securities, LLC CMA in March 2025, signaling a move away from prior core operations.
  • Brand and Market Presence: Rebranded to Chaince Digital Holdings (ticker CD) in November 2025 and launched a new website to support strategic repositioning and client outreach.
  • Geographic and Operational Expansion: Opened New York operations to serve U.S. clients and established Chaince (Shenzhen) Consulting in August 2025 to support APAC business development.
  • Discontinued Operations — Mining: Board approved wind-down and sale of Filecoin mining assets in December 2025; mining activities were classified as discontinued operations.

Original SEC Filing: Chaince Digital Holdings Inc. [ CD ] - 10-K - Mar. 26, 2026

Disclaimer

data.title: Noveris Provides Announces Trading Halt Order, And Clarification As To Article
data.description:
(TheNewswire)

Vancouver, British Columbia, March 26, 2026 –TheNewswire – NoverisHealth Sciences Inc. (the “Company”) (FSE: 0NF0) announces that the British Columbia Securities Commission
(“BCSC”) issued an order dated March 25, 2026 under section 89(1)of the SecuritiesAct (British Columbia) halting all trading inthe Company’s securities until April 16, 2026 (the “Order”)due to, among other matters, unexplained and unusualfluctuations in the volume of trading in, or market price, of theCompany’s shares traded on the CSE. The full text of the Order canbe viewed on www.sedarplus.ca.

The Company also wishes to clarify that the Company hasnot authorized, commissioned, or otherwise been involved in thecreation or distribution of an article concerning the Companycirculated by John Michaels on March 19, 2026 (the “Article”).  TheCompany further confirms that neither the Company nor its investorrelations service provider was aware of the Article prior to itspublication, and neither has any relationship or affiliation with theindividual or entities responsible for its authorship ordissemination. The Company did not provide information, commentary,review, approval, or input of any kind. Any statements, claims, oropinions expressed in the Article should not be construed as havingbeen reviewed, verified, endorsed, or supported by the Company.  Inparticular, the Article states that “the company isFDA-registered”.  To the extent “FDA” refers to U.S. Food andDrug Administration, this statement is inaccurate.  The Company doesnot have any registrations with the Food and Drug Administration, noris actively pursuing any applications for registration for any of itsproducts at this time. The Company refers readers to its publicdisclosure, which remains current, and confirms that it has nomaterial undisclosed information at this time.

Noveris intends to cooperate fully with the BCSC, andto work diligently toward satisfying any conditions necessary for theresumption of trading of its common shares on the CSE. The Companywill provide further information as it becomes available.

NOVERIS HEALTH SCIENCES INC.

Jason Birmingham  

Chief Executive Officer  

Tel: +1 778-900-NVRS (6877)  

Investor Relations Email: fair@noveris.health  

Web: http://noveris.health

About Noveris Health Sciences Inc. 

The Company is a biotechnology company developing thenext generation of innovative medications and therapies to addressmental health disorders such as nicotine addiction and posttraumaticstress disorder (PTSD). The core strategy blends advanced technologywith an elaborate infrastructure for drug discovery and development.Noveris’ dedicated multinational team constantly develops new pathsfor breakthrough treatment solutions in areas with considerable unmet needs. By collaborating with some of theworld's leading specialists, the Company aspires to responsiblyspeed up the development of breakthrough medications to providepatients with safer and more effective treatment solutions. At thesame time, Noveris’ approach focuses on the next generation ofpsychedelic medicine by creating innovative compounds with unmatchedtherapeutic potential through its clinical trial efforts withworldclass scientific and regulatory expertise.  

NEITHER THE CSENOR ITS REGULATIONS SERVICES PROVIDER HAVE REVIEWED OR ACCEPTRESPONSIBILITY FOR THE ADEQUACY OR ACCURACY OF THIS RELEASE.

Copyright (c) 2026 TheNewswire - All rights reserved.

data.title: Identiv 2025 10-K: Revenue $21.5M, EPS $(0.79) from continuing operations
data.description:
Identiv reported fiscal 2025 net revenue of $21.5 million and a loss per share from continuing operations of $(0.79), reflecting a 19% revenue decline driven by lower unit sales and the strategic exit of low‑margin business. Gross profit improved to $1.3 million with gross margin rising to 6.1%, while operating loss from continuing operations was $(22.1) million as the company reduced operating expenses. The company repositioned around its IoT RFID business following the prior sale of its Physical Security business, with operational changes expected to drive margin improvement into 2026.

Financial Highlights

  • Net revenue: $21.5 million, down 19% versus 2024 due to lower unit sales and exit of low‑margin business.
  • Gross profit: $1.3 million (gross margin 6.1%), improved from $0.3 million in 2024.
  • Operating income (Loss from continuing operations): $(22.1) million; operating expenses reduced 17% year‑over‑year.
  • Net income (loss): $(18.0) million from continuing operations, compared with net income $74.8 million in 2024 (which included discontinued operations).
  • Net income per share: $(0.79) basic and diluted from continuing operations for 2025; $3.14 net (includes discontinued operations in 2024).

Business Highlights

  • Revenue trend and portfolio shift: Revenue declined 19% as the company exited low‑margin opportunities and experienced reduced volume from a large customer, while intentionally shifting focus to higher‑margin, complex RFID IoT devices.
  • Channel and market focus: Emphasis on specialty RFID IoT solutions for healthcare, logistics and luxury markets to pursue higher margins and more complex product opportunities.
  • Production transition: Completed consolidation of manufacturing to a single, state‑of‑the‑art Thailand facility in 2Q 2025 and closed the Singapore site by 4Q 2025 to improve efficiency and lower fixed overhead.
  • Post‑divestiture positioning: After the sale of the Physical Security business in September 2024, the company now operates a single IoT business segment focused on specialty RFID solutions.
  • Outlook on margins: Improved Thailand utilization and lower fixed overhead drove gross margin momentum in 2025, with management expecting further margin improvement in 2026.

Original SEC Filing: Identiv, Inc. [ INVE ] - 10-K - Mar. 26, 2026

Disclaimer
This is an AI-powered summary. It may contain inaccuracies. Consider verifying important information with the source. Please note this summary is solely based on documents filed with the SEC.

data.title: INNOVATE 10-K: Revenue $1,246.0M; Net loss $(64.0)M
data.description:
INNOVATE reported fiscal-year revenue of $1,246.0 million and a net loss of $(64.0) million, reflecting year-over-year revenue growth driven by its Infrastructure business but pressure on profitability from margin compression and higher interest expense. Operating income declined versus the prior year and net loss attributable to INNOVATE totaled $(60.6) million after non-controlling interests and taxes. The results highlight stronger top-line performance alongside increased financing costs and reduced operating leverage.

Financial Highlights

  • Total revenue: $1,246.0 million, up $138.9 million versus 2024, driven primarily by Infrastructure.
  • Gross profit: $219.4 million (reported)
  • Total income from operations: $28.7 million, down $11.3 million versus 2024 due to lower gross profit and other operating income.
  • Net loss: $(64.0) million, a deterioration of $24.3 million versus 2024 driven by higher interest expense.
  • Net loss attributable to INNOVATE: $(60.6) million (after non-controlling interests and taxes).

Business Highlights

  • Revenue drivers: Infrastructure revenue rose significantly and was the primary contributor to consolidated revenue growth year-over-year.
  • Channel and market dynamics: The Spectrum business experienced reduced direct-response demand and customer terminations, partially offset by new network launches.
  • Brand momentum: Life Sciences R2 Technologies increased international unit and consumable sales, boosting segment revenue.
  • Operational milestones: DBMG expanded backlog with large commercial projects and completed a major refinancing to support ongoing operations.
  • Product and regulatory progress: MediBeacon secured FDA and NMPA approvals for TGFR systems, enabling milestone conversions and potential partnership payments.

Original SEC Filing: INNOVATE Corp. [ VATE ] - 10-K - Mar. 26, 2026

Disclaimer

data.title: Twenty One Capital now 2nd-largest publicly traded BTC holder after MARA sale
data.description:
Jack Mallers’ Twenty One Capital is now the second-largest publicly traded Bitcoin treasury by holdings, after miner MARA sold off a portion of its holdings and fell to the number three spot.

The newly formed Bitcoin treasury company holds 43,514 BTC in its corporate treasury, valued at over $2.9 billion using the market price at the time of this writing, according to data from BitcoinTreasuries.

Twenty One Capital was publicly listed late last year following its business combination with Cantor Equity Partners, a special purpose acquisition company. Now trading under the ticker XXI, the NYSE-listed shares are down more than 25% year to date.

MARA sold 15,133 BTC, valued at about $1.1 billion, throughout March 2026. The next largest publicly traded Bitcoin holder is Japanese BTC treasury company Metaplanet with 35,100. Bitcoin Treasuries analyst Tyler Rowe in a note Thursday said: 

“For the industry, it's a cautionary signal. MARA borrowed aggressively to stack sats during the bull run and is now selling Bitcoin at a loss to service that debt. This is the precise scenario critics of debt-fueled treasury strategies have warned about.”

This aggressive borrowing is in “sharp contrast” to the business model popularized by BTC treasury company Strategy, which treats BTC as “perpetual digital credit,” using it as collateral to continually finance BTC acquisitions.

"Can miners sustainably operate as Bitcoin treasury companies without the capital markets infrastructure Saylor spent five years building," Rowe said in the note shared with Cointelegraph.

Some market observers note the change signals the capitulation of crypto treasury and mining companies amid a challenging business environment, worsened by the crypto bear market that started in October 2025 and declining share prices.

Analysts forecast the decline of the crypto treasury space in 2025

In June 2025, venture capital firm Breed said that only a few crypto treasury companies would survive the “death spiral” of contracting market net asset values (mNAVs) by maintaining a price premium that would allow these companies to secure more financing.

As access to cheap financing options disappears, companies trading at or below their net asset value would have to sell their BTC holdings to meet debt obligations, according to Breed.

Companies that treat their crypto holdings as a speculative bet, rather than a long-term play, were likely to capitulate between cycles, Deng Chao, CEO of asset manager HashKey Capital, told Cointelegraph. 

At the same time, crypto treasury companies with a disciplined treasury strategy would last through multiple cycles, he said.
